documentation.readme: Description ---- The LaZagne project is an open source application used to retrieve lots of passwords stored on a local computer. Each software stores its passwords using different techniques (plaintext, APIs, custom algorithms, databases, etc.). This tool has been developed for the purpose of finding these passwords for the most commonly-used software.
usage_sections: Launch only a specific software script
Write all passwords found into a file (-oN for Normal txt, -oJ for Json, -oA for All).
Quiet mode (nothing will be printed on the standard output)

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `laZagne.exe all`. Further invocations from upstream: `laZagne.exe browsers`; `laZagne.exe browsers -firefox`. trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need post_exploitation, use LaZagne because Description ---- The LaZagne project is an open source application used to retrieve lots of passwords stored on a local computer. Each software stores its passw.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `laZagne.exe all` as documented upstream for LaZagne.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `laZagne.exe browsers` as documented upstream for LaZagne.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `laZagne.exe browsers -firefox` as documented upstream for LaZagne.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `laZagne.exe all -oN` as documented upstream for LaZagne.
When chaining post_exploitation tools, pair LaZagne with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `LaZagne --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
